attributes.content:
## Overview

The Invitation card gives the workspace a structured home for access invitation with recipient, resource, permissions, expiry, and acceptance status.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Invitation anchors identity, permissions, or relationship context. It becomes most useful when other cards can link to it consistently, query against it safely, and inherit its context without duplicating sensitive or fast-changing information.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Credential]]** -- Invitation may depend on managed access or secure configuration stored separately.
- **[[Integration]]** -- Invitation can sync with or depend on external systems through the shared integration layer.
- **[[Tool Definition]]** -- Tool calls can read from or write to Invitation when automation needs structured side effects.
- **[[Trigger]]** -- Changes to Invitation can fire automation, and automation can also create or update Invitation.
- **[[Pass / Wallet Item]]** -- Pass / Wallet Item can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Invitation.
- **[[API Key]]** -- API Key can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Invitation.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Invitation as the structured home for access invitation with recipient, resource, permissions, expiry, and acceptance status and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Invitation when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Invitation stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Invitation
cardInfo.summary: Access invitation with recipient, resource, permissions, expiry, and acceptance status.
